prompt:
While researching a historic theater restoration, a student took the following notes:
• The theater opened in 1912.
• A fire damaged it badly in 1980.
• Donors funded a full restoration this year.

The student wants to present the building's troubled history. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: A theater, a building made for staging plays and concerts, often serves as a gathering place for a whole community.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not present the building's troubled history as the prompt asks.

choices.text: When the theater first opened its doors to audiences back in 1912, it quickly became a centerpiece of the town.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not present the building's troubled history as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Thanks to a group of generous donors, the theater received a complete restoration during the course of this past year.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not present the building's troubled history as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Opened in 1912, the theater was nearly destroyed by a fire in 1980 before donors finally funded a full restoration this year.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to present the building's troubled history.

explanation: The goal is to present the building's troubled history; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a tornado warning system, a student took the following notes:
• New radar detects rotation earlier.
• Alerts now arrive minutes sooner.
• Earlier warnings save lives.

The student wants to stress the life-saving value of earlier alerts. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Thanks to the upgraded system, the warnings now reach people in the storm's path several minutes ahead of before.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not stress the life-saving value of earlier alerts as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The recently installed radar is able to pick up the rotation inside a storm earlier than the older equipment could.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not stress the life-saving value of earlier alerts as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A tornado, a violently spinning column of air, can flatten buildings within seconds of touching down on the ground.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not stress the life-saving value of earlier alerts as the prompt asks.

choices.text: By detecting rotation earlier and sending alerts minutes sooner, the new radar gives families precious extra time that saves lives.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to stress the life-saving value of earlier alerts.

explanation: The goal is to stress the life-saving value of earlier alerts; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a coral nursery, a student took the following notes:
• Divers grow coral fragments on underwater frames.
• Fragments are replanted on damaged reefs.
• Survival rates exceed 70%.

The student wants to explain the method and its success. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Coral reefs, which shelter a quarter of all marine species, are among the most important ecosystems in the ocean.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain the method and its success as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Divers grow coral fragments on underwater frames and replant them on damaged reefs, where more than 70% survive.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to explain the method and its success.

choices.text: Working below the surface, divers carefully grow small coral fragments on specially built underwater frames.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain the method and its success as the prompt asks.

choices.text: More than 70% of the transplanted coral fragments survive, a rate the nursery's divers consider a genuine success.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain the method and its success as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to explain the method and its success; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching an electric bus rollout, a student took the following notes:
• The agency replaced 50 diesel buses.
• The fleet now runs nearly silent.
• Riders report a smoother, cleaner ride.

The student wants to describe how the change affected riders. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Now that 50 diesel buses have been replaced, riders enjoy a fleet that runs nearly silent and gives a smoother, cleaner ride.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to describe how the change affected riders.

choices.text: In a major shift, the transit agency went ahead and replaced fifty of its aging diesel buses with new models.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not describe how the change affected riders as the prompt asks.

choices.text: An electric bus, powered by rechargeable batteries instead of fuel, produces no exhaust as it travels its route.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not describe how the change affected riders as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The new electric buses move so quietly that pedestrians sometimes fail to hear one approaching the curb.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not describe how the change affected riders as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to describe how the change affected riders; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a coral spawning event, a student took the following notes:
• The corals spawn one night each year.
• They release eggs in sync with the full moon.
• Divers collect the eggs to raise new colonies.

The student wants to convey the remarkable timing of the spawning. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: On the night of the spawning, divers carefully gather the floating eggs in order to raise brand-new colonies.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not convey the remarkable timing of the spawning as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Coral, a tiny animal that builds a stony skeleton, joins with countless others to form a sprawling living reef.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not convey the remarkable timing of the spawning as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Cued by the full moon, an entire reef of corals releases its eggs on a single synchronized night each year.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to convey the remarkable timing of the spawning.

choices.text: The eggs that the corals release during the event are collected and later used to grow fresh coral colonies.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not convey the remarkable timing of the spawning as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to convey the remarkable timing of the spawning; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a peat bog excavation, a student took the following notes:
• The bog preserved a 4,000-year-old canoe.
• Low oxygen kept the wood from rotting.
• The find reveals ancient travel routes.

The student wants to explain why the canoe survived so long. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: A bog, a soft wetland built up from layers of decaying plants, forms slowly over many thousands of years.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain why the canoe survived so long as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The well-preserved canoe pulled from the bog offers fresh clues about the routes ancient people once traveled.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain why the canoe survived so long as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Buried deep in the peat, the canoe turned out to be roughly four thousand years old when experts examined it.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain why the canoe survived so long as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Because the bog's waterlogged, low-oxygen mud kept the wood from rotting, a canoe survived there for some 4,000 years.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to explain why the canoe survived so long.

explanation: The goal is to explain why the canoe survived so long; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a public seed library, a student took the following notes:
• Gardeners borrow seeds for free.
• They return seeds saved from their harvest.
• The collection favors local heirloom varieties.

The student wants to explain how the library sustains itself. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: The collection housed at the seed library leans heavily toward heirloom varieties suited to the local climate.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain how the library sustains itself as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A seed, the small package from which a plant grows, can stay viable for years if it is stored properly.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain how the library sustains itself as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The seed library keeps going because gardeners who borrow seeds for free return new ones saved from their own harvests.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to explain how the library sustains itself.

choices.text: Any gardener in the community is welcome to borrow a packet of seeds from the library at no cost at all.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain how the library sustains itself as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to explain how the library sustains itself; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching an ancient road discovery, a student took the following notes:
• Archaeologists found a stone road buried under a field.
• The road is about 2,000 years old.
• It connected two former market towns.

The student wants to introduce the discovery to readers. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Archaeologists have uncovered a 2,000-year-old stone road, buried beneath a field, that once linked two market towns.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to introduce the discovery to readers.

choices.text: Archaeologists, the scientists who study the human past, often spend years carefully excavating sites buried under fields.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not introduce the discovery to readers as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The road that the archaeologists studied, now roughly 2,000 years old, must have seemed quite modern when it was first built.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not introduce the discovery to readers as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Long ago, the stone road connected two market towns whose traders likely carried goods back and forth along its surface.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not introduce the discovery to readers as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to introduce the discovery to readers; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a school orchard program, a student took the following notes:
• Students planted fruit trees on campus.
• They learn pruning and grafting by hand.
• The harvest is shared in the cafeteria.

The student wants to describe what students gain from the program. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: The fruit trees that the students planted now grow in neat rows across one corner of the school's campus.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not describe what students gain from the program as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Whatever fruit the young orchard manages to produce is gathered up and shared with everyone in the cafeteria.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not describe what students gain from the program as the prompt asks.

choices.text: An orchard, a planting of trees grown for their fruit, takes several patient years to reach full production.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not describe what students gain from the program as the prompt asks.

choices.text: By planting the campus trees and tending them by hand, students learn pruning and grafting while sharing the harvest at lunch.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to describe what students gain from the program.

explanation: The goal is to describe what students gain from the program; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a meteor shower, a student took the following notes:
• The shower peaks in mid-August each year.
• Viewers may see up to 60 meteors an hour.
• It is caused by debris from an old comet.

The student wants to explain what causes the shower. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Anyone hoping to catch the show should find a dark spot in mid-August and let their eyes adjust to the night sky.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain what causes the shower as the prompt asks.

choices.text: At its peak in mid-August, the shower can treat patient viewers to as many as sixty meteors every single hour.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain what causes the shower as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A meteor, often called a shooting star, is a streak of light made when a particle burns up in the atmosphere.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain what causes the shower as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The August shower occurs as Earth passes through the dusty trail of an old comet, where bits of debris burn up as meteors.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to explain what causes the shower.

explanation: The goal is to explain what causes the shower; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a museum lighting upgrade, a student took the following notes:
• New LED lights replaced old halogen bulbs.
• The lights give off far less heat.
• Fragile paintings now fade more slowly.

The student wants to emphasize the benefit to the artwork. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: As part of the upgrade, the museum swapped out all of its old halogen bulbs for modern, efficient LED lights.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the benefit to the artwork as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Because the new LED lights give off far less heat than the old halogen bulbs, the museum's fragile paintings now fade more slowly.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to emphasize the benefit to the artwork.

choices.text: Compared with the bulbs they replaced, the new lights installed throughout the galleries produce noticeably less heat.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the benefit to the artwork as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A museum, a place that collects and displays objects of interest, must balance public access with careful conservation.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the benefit to the artwork as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to emphasize the benefit to the artwork; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.
